title: Extracting Values From Numeric Data
text: Extracting Values From Numeric Data When you are working with data stored as a Numeric or Float value, you can extract information such as the integer part or the fractional part of a value. This extraction is done with a numeric extraction parameter. The format for using numeric extraction is: ( Field | Variable ) = Source [ Parameter ] Table 1. Numeric Extraction Parameters Parameter Data extracted trunc | I Integer part of the value. fraction | F Fractional part of the value. round {, n } | R Rounded value of a number, where n is an integer that indicates the decimal place to which you want to round the value. If n < 0, round to n positions left of the decimal point. if n > 0, round to n positions right of the decimal point. If n = 0 or omitted, round to the nearest unit. Note: If the fixed number of decimal digits after the decimal point is explicitly set to 0 in Source (that is, the number is an integer) it is not rounded, to preserve integer arithmetic. Example: Fraction and Truncate The following code shows the effect of numeric extraction parameters on a value. variables numeric vSource, vFraction, vTrunc, vCalc endvariables vSource = 123.76 vFraction = vSource [fraction] ; vFraction = 0.76 vTrunc = vSource [trunc] ; vTrunc = 123 vCalc = vSource[trunc]+.11 ; vCalc = 123.11 Copy Example: Rounding The following code shows the effect of the value of n on rounding: variables numeric vRounded , vRoundedA, vRoundedB, vRoundedC endvariables vSource = 1897.7652 vRounded = vSource[round] ; vRounded = 1898 vRoundedA = vSource[round,0] ; vRoundedA = 1898 vRoundedB = vSource[round,-2] ; vRoundedB = 1900 vRoundedC = vSource[round,2] ; vRoundedC = 1897.77 Copy Was this topic helpful?
